expectations.text: Output starts with '# 審查' header identifying this as user-provided content
expectations.evidence: Header is '# Biblical Fun-Facts Review Report' — English, no '審查' or user-provided identification.

expectations.text: Only checks 1-5 run (checks 6-10 skipped for pasted text)
expectations.evidence: No numbered checks at all. Uses free-form per-claim structure (## Claim 1 through ## Claim 4).

expectations.text: Check 1 (事實錯誤) flags the horse claim — Acts 9 does not mention a horse
expectations.evidence: Claim 1: 'nowhere in Acts 9, Acts 22, or Acts 26 — the three accounts of the Damascus road event — is a horse mentioned.'

expectations.text: Flags the whale claim — Jonah 1:17 says 大魚 (dag gadol) not 鯨魚
expectations.evidence: Claim 2: 'The Hebrew text of Jonah 1:17 uses דָּג גָּדוֹל (dag gadol), meaning "great fish," not "whale."'

expectations.text: Check 5 (數量計算) flags 十二條 as incorrect — should be ten
expectations.evidence: Claim 3: 'The Ten Commandments number ten, not twelve.'

expectations.text: Check 4 (過度簡化) flags the 'only human' conclusion as oversimplified
expectations.evidence: Claim 4: 'This is an editorialized interpretation presented as if it were a biblical fact.'

expectations.text: Finds at least 3 distinct issues across the 4 entries
expectations.evidence: Found 4 distinct issues, one per claim: horse, whale, twelve, oversimplification.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline found all content issues but lacked the structured format (Chinese headers, numbered checks, skipped 6-10).
